items.title: La Bourse de Casablanca ouvre en bonne mine
items.description: La Bourse de Casablanca a ouvert en bonne mine mardi, son indice phare, le MASI, s'adjugeant 0,92% à 17.701,39 points (pts). Le MASI 20, qui regroupe les 20 valeurs les plus liquides, prenait 0,94% à 1.313,38 pts et le MASI ESG, indice des entreprises ayant la meilleure notation ESG selon Moody's ESG Solutions, progressait de 1,1% à 1.257,45 pts. Le MASI Mid and Small Cap, qui reflète la performance des petites et moyennes capitalisations, se bonifiait de 0,7% à 1.760,3 pts. Aux valeurs individuelles, SMI (+6,64% à 6.292 DH), Microdata (+5,2% à 749 DH), Med Paper (+5,01% à 26,4 DH), Delta Holding (+3,75% à 54,99 DH) et Label'Vie (+3,52% à 3.646 DH) réalisaient les meilleures performances. À l'opposé, Zellidja (-8,89% à 164 DH), M2M Group (-3,73% à 361 DH), SGTM (-0,29% à 688 DH), SNEP (-0,29% à 313,1 DH) et S2M (-0,2% à 473,05 DH) accusaient les plus fortes baisses. La veille, le MASI avait clôturé sur une perte de 0,22%. The post La Bourse de Casablanca ouvre en bonne mine appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: New UK govt tackles cost of living with energy tax cut
items.description: Britain’s new Prime Minister Andy Burnham on Tuesday kickstarted his programme to ease the cost of living for millions of Britons by planning to remove tax on household electricity bills. The Labour leader, who took over as PM on Monday, said value added tax (VAT) would be taken off bills from October in a policy costing the government about £850 million ($1.143 billion) in the current UK financial year. “We’re taking immediate action to cut taxes on energy bills, put more money in people’s pockets and bring back hope,” Burnham said in a statement. The measure is a saving of about £45 a year on the average household bill, the government said. “The change supports the poorest households, who spend a higher percentage of their income on energy bills,” it added. The measure’s start date is due to coincide with an expected rise in the UK energy regulator’s price cap on electricity and gas prices, a consequence of the US-Iran war. The government said the tax cut would be funded by cancelling previous prime minister Keir Starmer’s plan for a digital ID scheme that would have cost the taxpayer £1.8 billion. The new PM has said he wishes to ease pressure also on small businesses, hit by the previous Labour administration’s tax hikes. At the same time, Burnham has pledged to respect the strict fiscal discipline seen under Starmer, whereby the government balances tax receipts with day-to-day state spending. – ‘Fiscal control’ – Official data Tuesday showed UK government borrowing dropped more than expected in June, handing a boost to new finance minister John Healey, who has replaced Rachel Reeves. Public sector net borrowing was £16 billion in June, down one third on a year earlier, the Office for National Statistics said in a statement. However, the ONS cautioned that “debt remains high by historical standards and close to the annual value of the entire UK economy”. Reacting to the data, Healey said “fiscal control is the first duty of any” finance minister. “And fiscal credibility is the bedrock for economic stability and for national security.” Healey piled pressure on Starmer last month when he announced he was stepping down as defence secretary in a stinging criticism of the government’s defence budget — and the Treasury, which he will now oversee. – Stable UK markets – UK markets were largely steady Tuesday having shown some signs of unease ahead of Healey’s appointment as chancellor of the exchequer. UK government bond rates had risen Monday after Burnham told British media there could be some “flexibility” allowed under the government’s balance-budget rules. “The prime minister and I have talked about how we will work in lockstep to meet the fiscal rules with a buffer against uncertainty and how we’ll make life more affordable for working people right across the UK,” Healey added. Separate official data Tuesday showed Britain’s unemployment rate unchanged at 4.9 percent. Burnham’s long-term goal is a “reindustrialisation” of Britain and mass building of municipal housing. While Britain’s economy has failed to take off since Labour returned to power two years ago, the International Monetary Fund forecasts it to outperform other major economies this year. The post New UK govt tackles cost of living with energy tax cut appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Tanzania’s President Samia to lead maternal, child health call at AU health summit in Accra
items.description: The African Union Champion for Maternal and Child Health, President Samia Suluhu Hassan of Tanzania, is expected to lead a major Call to Action on Maternal, Newborn, Child and Adolescent Survival during the AU Extraordinary Summit on Universal Health Coverage in Accra. The side event, which forms part of the summit programme, will be held in partnership with the Accra Reset and will focus on accelerating efforts to reduce preventable deaths among mothers, newborns, children and adolescents across Africa. The AU Extraordinary Session of the Assembly of Heads of State and Government, which begins on July 21, 2026, will bring together leaders, policymakers, health experts and development partners to discuss strategies for achieving stronger and more equitable health systems on the continent. The summit will also address critical health challenges including HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, maternal mortality, non-communicable diseases and neglected tropical diseases. President John Dramani Mahama, who is hosting the gathering, is expected to highlight Ghana’s healthcare reforms and investments aimed at expanding access to quality healthcare and advancing universal health coverage. According to the President’s Spokesperson and Minister for Government Communications, Felix Kwakye Ofosu, Ghana’s hosting of the summit reflects the country’s commitment to working with African Union member states to develop practical, people-centred solutions to improve health outcomes. The government has extended a welcome to Heads of State, delegates and stakeholders attending the summit, while reaffirming Ghana’s commitment to collaborating with partners to advance the shared goal of universal health coverage across Africa.

items.title: 3 excise officials kidnapped after attack on checkpost in Balochistan's Chagai: police sources
items.description: QUETTA: Three excise officials have been abducted at gunpoint following an attack on their department’s checkpost on the Dalbandin–Taftan Highway in Balochistan’s Chagai district, police sources told Dawn on Tuesday. According to the sources, the attack was carried out by unidentified men on Monday evening. They said around 30 armed men surrounded the checkpost that was set up temporarily for routine checking, held the officials on duty at gunpoint and later abducted them. The sources added that the attackers also took away two official vehicles and weapons. Police and other law enforcement personnel rushed to the site after being informed of the incident and cordoned off the area, the sources said, adding that a search operation has been launched to trace the attackers and ensure the safe recovery of the abducted officials. They said an investigation was under way to ascertain the identity of the assailants and determine the route they used to flee. The incident takes place as Balochistan continues to face security challenges. Earlier this month, security forces and law enforcement personnel jointly launched Operation Shaban in Balochistan in retaliation for a deadly attack on a police post at the Mangi Dam pumping station in Ziarat on July 6. During that assault, armed attackers kidnapped and killed 27 police officers . A monthly security assessment released in early June by the Pakistan Institute for Conflict and Security Studies (PICSS) said that after two consecutive months of improvement, Pakistan’s security situation deteriorated sharply in May, driven primarily by escalating terrorist violence in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa and Balochistan. The report said Balochistan emerged as the most affected province during the month. The province recorded 71 terrorist attacks, compared with 34 in April, representing a 109pc increase. The deteriorating security situation was further reflected in a surge in kidnappings, it said. Of the 54 abductions reported nationwide during May, 52 occurred in Balochistan alone, highlighting the growing operational reach and confidence of terrorist groups in the province, it said. On July 9, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said that the country’s civil and military leadership had taken a “mutual and singular decision ” to end terrorism after multiple major terrorist incidents in Balochistan. He made the remarks at a meeting in Quetta of the Provincial Apex Committee on the National Action Plan , with Chief of Defence Forces (CDF) and Chief of the Army Staff Field Marshal Asim Munir also present. PM Shehbaz’s statement came a day after the military Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR) Director General Lt Gen Ahmed Sharif Chaudhry addressed a press conference , pointing the finger squarely at India and Afghanistan for being behind recent attacks in Balochistan. He specifically mentioned “three major terrorist incidents” in recent days — an armed attack on the outskirts of Quetta on July 5, an assault on a police post in Ziarat on July 6, and an ambush of an army convoy in Bela on July 7.

items.title: Cameroun : la SRC clarifie les motifs du recouvrement de 7,4 millions de F lancé contre Rebecca Enonchong
items.description: © DR La Société de recouvrement de créances explique l'origine dudit recouvrement ainsi que son fondement dans une mise au point en date du 20 juillet 2026. D'après le document signé du directeur général de la SRC Marie Rose Messi, le recouvrement est lié à l'ouverture d'un compte auprès de l'Union Bank of Cameroon (UBC) et présentant un solde débiteur ancien. La société de recouvrement des créances vient par sa sortie apporter des précisions pour démentir certaines déclarations qui circulent sur les réseaux sociaux. Ces propos expliquent le recouvrement par une certaine créance créée à la suite d'une prise de position politique récente de la principale concernée, Rebecca Enonchong en l'occurrence. Et les mêmes sources tentent d’expliquer qu'aucun document n'existe sur ce dossier. Mais il n'en est rien selon la SRC. Non seulement elle explique l'origine de l'affaire, mais indique qu'il existe des pièces pour ce dossier-là. Lequel comprend les pièces d'identification et de fonctionnement du compte, le décompte de la créance, la clôture juridique du compte, la notification de cession, les sommations de payer ainsi que les actes de poursuite prévus par les textes en vigueur. Le recouvrement vise la société APPSTECH qui a comme dirigeante ou mandataire habilitée identifiée Mme Rebecca Enonchong. Elle a été notifiée pour le remboursement de la dette due à l'APPSTECH qui s'élève à 7 434 263 francs CFA. En sa qualité de dirigeante de cette société, la concernée est appelée à répondre de celle-ci et dispose du droit de contester la créance ou les actes d'exécution devant les juridictions compétentes. Ainsi, d'après ces précisions, il ne s'agit pas d'une affaire personnelle montée contre Rebecca Enonchong, femme d'impact qui ne cachent pas souvent ses prises de position contre le régime. Cet article Cameroun : la SRC clarifie les motifs du recouvrement de 7,4 millions de F lancé contre Rebecca Enonchong est apparu en premier sur Journalducameroun.com .

items.title: Bono East Police arrest six over highway robbery, recover GH¢300,000 stolen cash
items.description: The Bono East Regional Police Command has arrested six suspects in connection with a highway robbery on the Portor-Gulumpe Highway and recovered the full GH¢300,000 allegedly stolen from a businessman. According to the Police, the robbery, which occurred on the evening of July 19, 2026, was allegedly planned by the victim’s driver, who is accused of providing information to the other suspects. The incident occurred around 8:30 pm near the Portor Police Barrier within the Kintampo Police Division. The Police said officers stationed at the barrier responded swiftly after receiving intelligence that a robbery was underway. Upon reaching the scene, four suspects reportedly fled into a nearby bush, while officers arrested Abdul Sadat Ali, 24, who was driving a Toyota Voxy with registration number AS 1541-25. During preliminary investigations, Abdul Sadat Ali allegedly mentioned another suspect, Samad Issahaka, 24, who was driving a Kia Rhino with registration number AS 7337-23. Police subsequently arrested Samad Issahaka, leading to further investigations that allegedly uncovered the involvement of the victim’s driver. How the Robbery Unfolded According to police reports, the incident occurred at approximately 8:30 pm on the Portor-Gulumpe Highway, just a few meters from the Portor Police Barrier under the Kintampo Police Division. Acting on intelligence that a robbery was in progress, police personnel on duty at the barrier swiftly responded and moved to the scene. Upon arrival, four suspects fled into the bush, but officers managed to arrest Abdul Sadat Ali, a 24-year-old driver of a Toyota Voxy with registration number AS 1541-25. During preliminary interrogation, Abdul Sadat Ali implicated Samad Issahaka, also 24, the driver of a Kia Rhino with registration number AS 7337-23, as a member of the robbery gang. Police promptly arrested him. The Shocking Twist: An Inside Job Further investigations uncovered a disturbing conspiracy. Yahaya Seidu, 35, the owner of the Kia Rhino, had instructed his driver, Samad Issahaka, to convey him to Buipe to purchase cattle. Unbeknownst to the businessman, his driver knew he was carrying a large sum of money and conspired with five others to rob him on the highway. Armed with a toy pistol, the suspects trailed the Kia Rhino and its occupants from Offinso in the Ashanti Region, caught up with them after the Portor Police Barrier, and robbed them of GHC 300,000.00 in cash. Pursuit and Arrest of Fleeing Suspects Police counter-terrorism personnel, together with the local police patrol team, pursued the four fleeing suspects into the bush and successfully arrested all of them. They have been identified as: 1· Abdul Rahman Mashud, 21 2· Asigri Avoka, 25 3· Awini Stephen, 19 4· Faisal Salifu, 26 A search conducted on the suspects led to the retrieval of the entire GHC 300,000.00 robbed from the victim, a major relief for the businessman who lost nothing in the end. Suspects Confess During interrogation, the suspects admitted that they had been tipped off by suspect Samad Issahaka, the victim’s driver, and had trailed the Kia Rhino from Offinso before executing the robbery at Portor. All six suspects are currently in police custody and are assisting with ongoing investigations. Police Commendation and Assurance The Ghana Police Service has commended the alertness and professionalism of the personnel on duty, as well as the counter-terrorism team for their support in effecting the arrests. Addressing the media at the Regional Command in Techiman, the Bono East Regional Police Commander, DCOP Shaibu Sadique-Osei, assured the public of the Police Service’s continued commitment to ensuring the safety and security of all persons and property within the Bono East Region. “This was an inside job. The driver, knowing his boss was carrying that amount of money, conspired with five others to stage the robbery. They used a toy pistol, but their intent was deadly serious,” DCOP Sadique-Osei stated. “We are pleased that our men on the ground acted with speed and precision. Crime will not pay in this region.” The Police Service has urged the public to remain vigilant and to report any suspicious activities, especially when traveling with significant sums of money.

items.title: Cameroun : risques d’inondations à Douala, le maire de la ville prévient les habitants, mobilise les leaders d’opinion
items.description: © (DR) Le maire Roger Mabssa Ndine appelle à la responsabilité de tous pour éviter des dégâts susceptibles d’être causés durant la grande saison des pluies qui se poursuit. Les pluies diluviennes qui peuvent s’abattre sur la capitale économique du Cameroun durant les mois de juin à août 2026 ont provoqué l’activation du système d’alerte de la Communauté urbaine de Douala auprès de la population. Le maire de la ville, Roger Mbassa Ndine, rappelle aux habitants que pendant cette période, des inondations, débordements des eaux, des eaux de ruissellement peuvent causer d’importants dégâts dans la ville. A titre préventif, l’autorité municipale demande aux habitants de faire preuve de vigilance, de limiter les déplacements non essentiels en cas de fortes pluies, d’éviter toute traversée des eaux de ruissellement. Des habitants gagneraient aussi à s’éloigner des zones à risque, à veiller en particulier à la sécurité des enfants, des personnes âgées, des personnes vulnérables. Il mobilise les leaders d’opinion pour relayer le message de sensibilisation et pour promouvoir les actions citoyennes de nettoyage, de curage et d’entretien des abords des habitations et des quartiers. Le maire appelle ainsi à l’action les chefferies traditionnelles, les chefs de communautés, les leaders des quartiers, d’associations, des comités locaux de développement ainsi que les forces vives de la ville. Leur activité va se joindre aux opérations de du curage, d’entretien des drains, de désobstruction des ouvrages d’assainissement et de dégagement des exécutoires. D’après les prévisions de l’Observatoire national sur les changements climatiques de la deuxième décade du mois de juillet, la ville de Douala est exposée aux orages, aux pluies torrentielles, occasionnelles et successives de longue durée associées à des problèmes de drainage. Ces pluies peuvent être à l’origine des inondations et des glissements de terrain. Ces derniers jours, deux immeubles se sont effondrés dans la ville entrainant la mort d’au moins sept personnes. Cet article Cameroun : risques d’inondations à Douala, le maire de la ville prévient les habitants, mobilise les leaders d’opinion est apparu en premier sur Journalducameroun.com .

items.title: Comienza la tercera ola de calor del verano, con lo peor en el este y en el sur de la Península
items.description: La tercera ola de calor en lo que va de este tórrido verano de 2026 comienza este martes 21 de julio y se extenderá hasta el jueves 23, según avanza Rubén del Campo, portavoz de la Agencia Estatal de Meteorología (Aemet). La buena noticia es que no será larga ―tres días es el mínimo para que un episodio cálido sea considerado ola― ni tampoco especialmente intensa ―no hay avisos rojos, de momento―, pero sí afectará a buena parte del territorio peninsular y Baleares. Del calor solo se librarán la cornisa cantábrica y Canarias, mientras que las zonas más castigadas “serán el este y sur de la Península, donde puntualmente podrán alcanzarse o incluso superarse los 44°”, explica Del Campo, una cota alejada del récord nacional de calor, que está en 47,6° . items.title: ATU launches Business School, urges stronger industry-academia partnership
items.description: Accra Technical University (ATU) has officially launched its Business School, transforming the former Faculty of Business into a fully-fledged school as part of efforts to strengthen business education and prepare graduates for the demands of the modern workplace. The launch brought together academics, industry leaders and stakeholders, who stressed the need for tertiary institutions to complement classroom learning with practical skills and real-world experience. Speaking at the event, internationally recognised marketing scholar and educator, Professor Robert Ebo Hinson, urged universities and businesses to adopt innovative approaches that respond to the changing dynamics of the global business environment. He introduced his self-developed BATTLE Framework, a strategic model aimed at helping institutions strengthen leadership, promote innovation and develop world-class standards of excellence. Professor Hinson emphasised the importance of rethinking traditional approaches to education and ensuring that graduates are equipped with skills relevant to contemporary industry needs. Chief Executive Officer of the McDan Group of Companies, Daniel McKorley, also called on tertiary institutions to prioritise practical training and industry exposure before students enter the job market. He said the corporate environment requires more than academic qualifications, stressing that graduates must be able to apply their knowledge to solve real-world problems. According to Mr McKorley, businesses do not operate on a “chew and pour” system, and students must acquire hands-on experience alongside theoretical understanding. The launch of the ATU Business School forms part of the university’s broader strategy to promote practical education, entrepreneurship, innovation and stronger partnerships between academia and industry. The new school is expected to expand ATU’s capacity to produce graduates who are academically prepared and professionally equipped to compete in an increasingly demanding job market. University officials said the initiative reflects ATU’s commitment to bridging the gap between academic training and industry expectations while contributing to Ghana’s human capital development.

items.title: NPP-USA faces internal dispute over disqualification of 5 aspirants ahead of branch elections
items.description: Crisis has emerged within the United States Branch of the New Patriotic Party (NPP-USA) following the disqualification of five aspirants ahead of the branch’s upcoming internal elections. The affected aspirants, who were contesting various executive positions, include Elijah Amanor Ametei, Branch Secretary hopeful; Nana Akua Musah, Youth Organiser hopeful; Ali Inusah Adams, Nasara Coordinator hopeful; Atta Johnson, Second Vice Chairman hopeful; and Stephen Adjei, Treasurer hopeful. According to sources close to the aspirants, the NPP-USA Election Committee cited non-payment of membership dues as the basis for their disqualification. However, the affected candidates have rejected the claim, insisting that they paid their dues within the required period and have documentary evidence, including receipts, to support their position. The aspirants are said to have presented the evidence to the Election Committee, but the decision to disqualify them was maintained. The development has since generated concerns among some party members, with critics alleging that the decision could undermine the fairness and credibility of the electoral process. Some supporters of the affected aspirants have called for the intervention of the party’s national leadership, including the Steering Committee, National Executive Committee and National Council, to review the matter and ensure that due process is followed. They argue that a failure to address the concerns could affect unity within the NPP-USA branch and impact the party’s organisational activities among its members in the diaspora.

items.title: Greater Accra records 1,054 vehicle interceptions in police crackdown on illegal sirens
items.description: The Ghana Police Service has intercepted 1,054 vehicles in the Greater Accra Region as part of an ongoing nationwide enforcement exercise targeting the unauthorised use of sirens, horns, strobe lights, beacon lights and improper vehicle modifications. The exercise, which forms part of efforts to improve road safety and enforce compliance with the Road Traffic Regulations, has also resulted in the removal and seizure of unauthorised devices and modifications found on some vehicles. The Police said drivers whose vehicles were found to be in breach of the regulations were educated on the relevant laws and advised to comply with the requirements governing vehicle use on Ghana’s roads. The Service disclosed that a total of 4,627 vehicles have been intercepted nationwide since the enforcement exercise began on June 15, 2026. The Police assured the public that the operation would continue across the country as part of measures to promote discipline among road users, enhance safety and ensure strict adherence to traffic regulations.

items.title: FCT farmers lament delay in release of 6,800 bags of fertiliser
items.description: By Gabriel Ewepu, Abuja Farmers in the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), under the auspices of the All Farmers Association of Nigeria (AFAN) FCT Chapter, have expressed concern over the alleged delay in the release of 6,800 bags of free fertiliser meant for the 2026 wet season farming. The fertilisers were reportedly distributed under the National Agricultural Development Fund’s Renewed Hope Farm Input Support Programme (FISP). Speaking with Vanguard, the Chairman of AFAN FCT Chapter, Dr Perpetual Okafor, said the farmers are becoming increasingly agitated as the farming season advances without access to the inputs. According to her, the farmers submitted their names as beneficiaries, but the fertilisers have not been released despite several visits and calls to the FCT Agriculture and Rural Development Secretariat. Okafor noted that the flag-off for the fertiliser distribution was held on June 15, 2026, and she visited the Department of Agri-services on June 17, 2026, but was told the Mandate Secretary was not available to sign the release. She said she has made repeated calls and sent messages to the Mandate Secretary without success. “It is very concerning that the fertilisers have not been released. The wet season farming will soon be over, and the fertilisers will no longer be useful to the farmers if not released on time,” she stated. Dr Okafor disclosed that some farmers have resorted to buying fertilisers from the open market to continue their farming activities. She gave an ultimatum to the Secretariat to release the fertilisers this week, warning that failure to do so may compel the farmers to take further action. The FCT AFAN boss appealed to the relevant authorities to expedite the release of the 6,800 bags of fertiliser to enable the 1,700 beneficiary farmers to benefit from the programme as intended. As of the time of filing this report, efforts to get a response from the FCT Agriculture and Rural Development Secretariat were unsuccessful. The post FCT farmers lament delay in release of 6,800 bags of fertiliser appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Réseaux sociaux en France : une loi pour interdire l'accès aux moins de 15 ans
items.description: Le Parlement doit adopter ce mardi une loi visant à interdire l'accès des moins de 15 ans aux réseaux sociaux les plus nocifs. Une mesure de protection des mineurs qui impose aussi de nouvelles contraintes aux géants du numérique.

items.title: Réseaux sociaux interdits aux moins de 15 ans : Emmanuel Macron, pionnier de la censure européenne
items.description: Les parlementaires ont valide l'interdiction des reseaux sociaux aux moins de 15 ans avec l'imposition aux plateformes d'une verification de l'âge. L’accord des parlementaires sur l’interdiction des réseaux sociaux aux moins de 15 ans est une étape majeure pour la protection des enfants en ligne. Cette ligne claire portée par le président de la République @EmmanuelMacron trouve son aboutissement parlementaire après des… — Clara Chappaz (@ClaraChappaz) July 20, 2026 Une mesure qui reflete les preoccupations croissantes sur les effets nefastes des reseaux sociaux sur la sante mentale des adolescents mais qui suscite egalement de vives critiques en matiere de controle social. Une avancee protectrice ou une censure deguisee ? Portee initialement par la deputee Renaissance Laure Miller et soutenue personnellement par Emmanuel Macron depuis plusieurs annees, cette proposition de loi a abouti a un accord en commission mixte paritaire le 20 juillet. Le texte devrait etre adopte definitivement ce 21 juillet, avec une entree en vigueur progressive des le 1er septembre pour les nouveaux comptes, et au 1er janvier 2027 pour les comptes existants. Sur X, la ministre deleguee chargee de l'intelligence artificielle et du numerique, Anne Le Henanff, s'est felicitee : « La France ouvre la voie […] Demain, la France sera le premier pays d'Europe a instaurer une majorite numerique pour mieux proteger nos enfants en ligne ». Majorité numérique : la France ouvre la voie. Je me félicite de l’accord trouvé par les parlementaires en CMP sur l’interdiction des réseaux sociaux aux moins de 15 ans. Demain, la France sera le premier pays d’Europe à instaurer une majorité numérique pour mieux protéger nos… — Anne Le Hénanff (@ALehenanff) July 20, 2026 Le dispositif cible TikTok, Snapchat, Instagram, Facebook et les fonctionnalites sociales de plateformes hybrides comme YouTube ou Roblox. Les messageries privees comme WhatsApp restent accessibles. Les plateformes devront recourir a des systemes de verification d'âge robustes, via des tiers de confiance (jetons anonymes bases sur France Identite ou d'autres outils), tout en respectant le DSA europeen . Cette initiative s'inspire de l'Australie, premier pays a avoir franchi le pas pour les moins de 16 ans . Malgre les defis techniques et les contournements possibles via VPN, les premiers bilans australiens montrent une reduction notable des jeunes utilisateurs. Des voix critiques se sont elevees. Le depute socialiste Arthur Delaporte a exprime des reserves : le texte constitue selon lui « une loi de transition, imparfaite », soulevant des questions constitutionnelles sur le droit a l'information des mineurs. 🏛️ Interdiction des réseaux sociaux aux moins de 15 ans dès la rentrée 🗣️ @ArthurDelaporte , député du Calvados et porte-parole du groupe socialiste : « L’interdiction est quasiment inapplicable. On ne sait toujours pas comment contrôler l’âge de manière effective alors que… pic.twitter.com/sP3WgHKzYw — Sud Radio (@SudRadio) July 20, 2026 Les Insoumis, via Louis Boyard, denoncent une « loi de fin d'anonymat sur Internet ». a l'inverse, le Rassemblement national a apporte son soutien, participant au consensus large hors opposition de LFI et abstention probable des socialistes. Emmanuel Macron, implique depuis 2017, transforme ainsi une priorite nationale en vitrine europeenne. Reste a voir si les plateformes se conformeront efficacement, sous peine de sanctions lourdes jusqu'a 6 % de leur chiffre d'affaires.

items.title: We’ve decided to reward Tinubu, Ododo with our votes – Kogi people
items.description: By Soni Daniel, Editor, Northern Region For empowering the Kogi State Governor, Ahmed Usman Ododo, to undertake critical projects that have improved their lives, the Kogi people have decided to re-elect President Ahmed Tinubu and the governor of the state in the next election. The people of the state said they felt a sense of belonging with the various federal and state projects, including roads, schools, hospitals and other vital infrastructure that have added value to their wellbeing. The Kogi State Commissioner for Environment and Ecological Management, Dr. Joseph Oluwasegun, gave the commitment in Yagba West while receiving member of the Presidential Media team and Renewed Hope Ambassadors, who toured federal and state projects in the crucial Kogi community. Oluwasegun recall that the Tinubu administration had enabled the Kogi governor to construct 15 kilometres of internal roads in Egbe and appoint two commissioners and other top government functionaries from the area. The commissioner said the Egbe area of Kogi has also been visited twice by Governor Ododo, something that was unprecedented in the history of the community. Oluwasegun said: “We are here to report that for the first time in the history of Egbe, governor Ododo has visited us twice and given us many roads, hospitals and schools. We are indeed very grateful for all these and more. We have never had it so good this community. “We cannot talk about the governor without appreciating President Tinubu who has given him more funds to work for us and also created the North Central Development Commission to accelerate our development. “We want to appreciate the two leaders with our votes in the next elections and our people will do it enmass and make them return to their respective offices,” the commissioner said. The Chairman of Yagba West LGA, Ologun Tosin, noted with satisfaction the speedy construction of the 15 kilometres of roads in Egbe, which has reduced travel time and increase the safety of the indigenes and praised both Tinubu and Ododo for the work done in the area. “We the people of Egbe will show them the depth of our appreciation when we go to vote in the coming elections,” Tosin stated. The DG of the Kogi State Road Maintenance Agency, Eng. Bolaji Taye, said that though the roads were done through direct labour to save cost, they were done to meet international standards to last. The Renewed Hope Ambassadors led by Chief Sunday Dare, the Special Adviser to the President on Public Communication, said the president was serious about providing critical infrastructure across the country and assured the people of Kogi that is keen on transforming the state. Chief Dare said that it was in a bid to provide functional roads to ease the movement of people and goods that the Tinubu administration recently awarded new contracts for the construction of more roads in Kogi and other states. While inspecting portions of the Kabba-Egbe-Omuaran Federal Highway in Kogi, the Senior Special Adviser to the President on Public Engagement, Mr. Fredrick Nwabufo, said that President was mindful of the need to make critical roads in the country to work and serve Nigerians effectively. “That is why Mr. President has turned many part of the country into construction sites to ensure that there are good roads for Nigerians,” Nwabufo said. Kogi State Information Commissioner, Mr. Kingsley Fanwo, lauded the Tinubu administration for awarding the contract for the road, which he said had collapsed and cut off the users before his intervention. Members of the RHA are in Kogi to inspect federal and state projects undertaken so far. The post We’ve decided to reward Tinubu, Ododo with our votes – Kogi people appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Législatives : qui ne pourra plus se présenter ? Les nouvelles règles qui redessinent l'accès au Parlement
items.description: À quelques semaines des prochaines élections législatives, les conditions d'éligibilité à la Chambre des représentants sont revues et endurcies. Avec l'adoption de la loi organique n°53.25 modifiant la loi relative à la Chambre des représentants, le législateur élargit la liste des personnes interdites de candidature, dans un objectif affiché de moralisation de la vie politique et de consolidation de la confiance des citoyens envers les institutions élues. Ce texte, dont plusieurs dispositions avaient suscité un vif débat lors de leur examen parlementaire, introduit de nouveaux cas d'inéligibilité tout en renforçant les sanctions applicables à certains élus et responsables publics. La Cour constitutionnelle a, pour sa part, validé ces nouvelles mesures, estimant qu'elles étaient conformes à la Constitution. Une liste élargie des personnes frappées d'inéligibilité La principale évolution concerne les candidats poursuivis en flagrant délit pour des infractions portant atteinte à la probité, à la moralité, à l'intégrité ou à la sincérité des opérations électorales. Le nouveau dispositif prévoit qu'une personne surprise en flagrant délit entre l'ouverture du dépôt des candidatures et le jour du scrutin ne pourra pas briguer un siège à la Chambre des représentants. Si le flagrant délit intervient pendant la période de dépôt des candidatures, sa liste est rejetée. Si les faits surviennent après la clôture du dépôt, mais avant le vote, la candidature est tout simplement annulée. La réforme vise également les personnes condamnées en appel pour des infractions entraînant la perte de leurs droits électoraux, ainsi que celles condamnées en première instance pour crime. Dans ce dernier cas, le jugement produit immédiatement ses effets sur l'éligibilité, le législateur estimant que la gravité des faits justifie une exclusion sans attendre l'épuisement des voies de recours. Sont également concernés les élus ayant fait l'objet d'une décision définitive de révocation de leur mandat. La Cour constitutionnelle valide le dispositif Ces nouvelles restrictions avaient alimenté les critiques de plusieurs parlementaires, certains y voyant une atteinte au principe de la présomption d'innocence. La Cour constitutionnelle a toutefois tranché en faveur du législateur. Dans sa décision, elle rappelle que les conditions d'éligibilité relèvent de l'organisation des droits politiques, domaine dans lequel la Constitution reconnaît au Parlement un large pouvoir d'appréciation. Selon la Cour, ces mesures ne constituent pas une sanction pénale mais un mécanisme préventif destiné à préserver l'intégrité du processus électoral. Elles n'affectent ni les droits de la défense ni les garanties d'un procès équitable, qui continuent de s'appliquer devant les juridictions compétentes. L'objectif affiché est de garantir la crédibilité des élections et de protéger la confiance dans l'institution parlementaire. Sanctions renforcées contre les élus révoqués La réforme alourdit également les conséquences pour les élus ayant été définitivement révoqués de leurs fonctions. Jusqu'à présent, une telle décision interdisait de se représenter durant un seul mandat électoral. Désormais, cette période d'inéligibilité est portée à deux mandats électoraux complets, traduisant la volonté des pouvoirs publics d'écarter durablement les responsables sanctionnés pour des manquements graves de la gestion des affaires publiques. Le texte modifie aussi les règles applicables à plusieurs catégories de hauts fonctionnaires. La durée pendant laquelle certains responsables de l'administration ne peuvent se présenter à une élection nationale est doublée, passant d'un à deux ans après la cessation de leurs fonctions. Quant à l'interdiction de se présenter dans les circonscriptions où ils exerçaient leurs responsabilités, elle est portée de deux à quatre ans. La réforme intègre en outre les cadres et fonctionnaires relevant du ministère de l'Intérieur parmi les catégories concernées par ces restrictions, dans le but de préserver la neutralité de l'administration territoriale et de garantir l'égalité entre les candidats. Fin d'une incompatibilité instaurée en 2021 À l'inverse, la loi supprime une disposition introduite lors des élections de 2021, qui interdisait le cumul entre un mandat de député et la présidence d'un conseil préfectoral, provincial ou d'une commune de plus de 300.000 habitants. Le législateur justifie cette suppression par la volonté de permettre à des élus disposant d'une expérience dans la gestion territoriale de continuer à contribuer au travail législatif, en misant davantage sur la complémentarité entre responsabilités locales et nationales. Avec cette réforme, le cadre juridique des prochaines législatives évolue sensiblement. Entre durcissement des conditions d'éligibilité et assouplissement de certaines incompatibilités, le législateur semble poursuivre une double ambition : renforcer l'exigence de probité dans l'accès aux responsabilités électives tout en élargissant le vivier des profils susceptibles de siéger au Parlement. The post Législatives : qui ne pourra plus se présenter ? Les nouvelles règles qui redessinent l'accès au Parlement appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Trump impose des droits de douane de 50% sur plusieurs produits canadiens
items.description: Le president americain, Donald Trump, a annonce l'instauration de droits de douane de 50 % sur differentes categories de produits canadiens, du vin aux crosses de hockey et au ciment. Selon la Maison Blanche , cette decision repond a ce que Washington considere comme un traitement « discriminatoire » reserve aux produits americains. L'administration americaine explique que cette mesure vise a compenser les desavantages subis par le commerce des etats-Unis et a retablir des conditions de concurrence plus « equitables » pour plusieurs secteurs strategiques, notamment l'automobile, les boissons alcoolisees et les produits laitiers. En revanche, les nouvelles taxes ne s'appliqueront pas aux produits energetiques, aux engrais potassiques, au poisson ni aux mineraux juges essentiels. Elles concerneront toutefois des marchandises qui etaient jusqu'a present protegees par l'accord commercial entre les etats-Unis, le Mexique et le Canada. La Maison Blanche affirme egalement que cette initiative a pour objectif de tenir le Canada responsable de pratiques commerciales qualifiees d'« injustifiees » et d'« inequitables » qui penaliseraient les travailleurs et les entreprises americains. Selon Associated Press, ces restrictions toucheront la majorite des produits canadiens. De son cote, un haut responsable de l'administration americaine a declare au Financial Times que seuls la Chine et le Canada avaient adopte des mesures de retorsion contre les etats-Unis au debut de la politique commerciale menee par Donald Trump. Le quotidien britannique estime que cette decision pourrait ouvrir une nouvelle phase de tensions commerciales en Amerique du Nord. Ces dernieres semaines, Donald Trump a multiplie les critiques envers le Canada. Le 13 juillet, il a accuse les autorites canadiennes de ne pas assurer le nettoyage des dechets dans les zones forestieres, estimant que cette situation contribuait a la pollution de l'air aux etats-Unis. Il a alors menace d'augmenter les droits de douane afin de contraindre Ottawa a « assumer les consequences » de cette situation. a la fin du mois de janvier, le chef d'etat americain avait egalement averti que si le Canada concluait un accord commercial avec la Chine, tous les produits canadiens importes aux etats-Unis seraient soumis a des droits de douane de 100 %. Il avait par ailleurs qualifie le Premier ministre canadien, Mark Carney, de « gouverneur ». Depuis le debut de son second mandat, Donald Trump a progressivement renforce les barrieres douanieres visant le Canada, dont 75 % des exportations sont destinees au marche americain. Apres un taux de 25 % applique a la majorite des produits a partir de mars 2025, les droits de douane generaux avaient ete releves a 35 % le 1er août 2025. Les nouvelles mesures a 50 % viennent desormais alourdir la pression sur certaines categories de produits canadiens.

items.title: 80 CSOs urge government to release full Constitutional Review Committee report
items.description: A coalition of 80 civil society organisations operating under the Citizens’ Platform on Constitutional Reforms (CPCR) has welcomed the government’s progress on constitutional reforms but is calling for the immediate publication of the full Constitutional Review Committee (CRC) report to promote transparency and meaningful public engagement. In a statement issued on July 20, 2026, the coalition described Cabinet’s announcement that the Attorney General and Minister for Justice will publish the government’s position paper on the CRC’s recommendations this week as “an important milestone” in the ongoing constitutional review process. “As Ghanaians prepare to receive government’s position on the CRC proposals, the Citizens’ Platform reiterates its call for the publication of the full report of the CRC to enable informed discussion and constructive engagement,” the statement said. The Platform recalled concerns it raised in a June 12 statement about the limited time available to implement constitutional reforms before Ghana enters another political and electoral cycle. It noted that it had previously called for the urgent publication of the CRC report, a clear implementation roadmap with timelines and milestones, the inauguration of the Constitutional Review Implementation Committee, and the publication of a legislative and referendum calendar in line with Articles 289 to 291 of the 1992 Constitution. According to the coalition, the imminent publication of the government’s position paper should accelerate these outstanding processes. The CPCR stressed that constitutional reform should remain a national, rather than partisan, undertaking, arguing that government’s response to the committee’s recommendations should be made public and subjected to broad national debate. “The credibility and legitimacy of the constitutional reform process will depend not only on the contents of the position paper but also on the transparency, inclusiveness and accountability of the process itself,” the Platform stated. The Constitutional Review Committee, chaired by Professor Henry Kwasi Prempeh, submitted its final report to President John Dramani Mahama on December 22, 2025, after conducting nationwide consultations on possible amendments to the 1992 Constitution. Among the committee’s reported recommendations are extending the presidential term from four to five years, separating the roles of ministers and Members of Parliament, establishing an independent Emoluments Commission, and reforming the appointment process for Metropolitan, Municipal and District Chief Executives (MMDCEs). President Mahama has consistently maintained that the review seeks to strengthen—not replace—the 1992 Constitution, which he has described as one of Ghana’s most enduring democratic achievements. Under the next phase of the process, Cabinet is expected to approve and publish the government’s position paper before an implementation committee is established to oversee the reforms. The Attorney General’s Department is also expected to harmonise the government’s position with the CRC’s recommendations before the proposals are forwarded to the Constitutional Review Implementation Committee. Meanwhile, Majority Leader Mahama Ayariga has indicated that constitutional amendment bills will be laid before Parliament during the current sitting, with both entrenched and non-entrenched provisions expected to be considered. The CPCR has encouraged citizens, political parties and other stakeholders to actively participate in the reform process by engaging with the proposed changes, implementation timelines and mechanisms for building consensus. The Platform reaffirmed its commitment to supporting what it described as “an open, inclusive, and citizen-centred constitutional reform process.” The Citizens’ Platform on Constitutional Reforms is a coalition facilitated by CDD-Ghana, STAR-Ghana Foundation and Democracy Hub, bringing together civil society organisations, activists and citizens to promote accountability and public participation in Ghana’s constitutional reform process. Press Statement – Citizens’ Platform Commends Government’s Progress on the Constitutional Review Proceess and Calls for Release of Full Report – 20-7-2026 Download

items.title: Centre committed to working closely with GB government, PM tells CM Amjad Hussain
items.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Tuesday met newly appointed Gilgit-Baltistan (GB) Chief Minister Advocate Amjad Hussain and assured him that the federal government was committed to working closely with his government for the welfare of the region’s people. According to the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO), the premier told Hussain that the Centre is committed to working with his government for “the welfare and well-being of the people of Gilgit-Baltistan”. The statement said that he also congratulated Hussain on assuming office. PM Shehbaz also said that the federal government was undertaking development initiatives in renewable energy, tourism, infrastructure, health, education and other sectors for the region’s progress. “Among the federal government’s projects, the top priorities include a 100-megawatt solar energy initiative and the establishment of knowledge schools,” the statement quoted the premier as saying. He added that the federal government was also planning to establish a Pakistan Asaan Khidmat Centre in Gilgit for the provision of convenient public services to the people. Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar, Minister for Planning Ahsan Iqbal and Minister for Economic Affairs Ahad Khan Cheema also attended the meeting. PPP’s Hussain was elected as the chief minister, unopposed, on June 22 by the GB Assembly. The PPP formed a government in GB with the support of the PML-N, mirroring the arrangement at the federal level, following the elections in early June.

items.title: "I Came to Iran to Debunk the Propaganda"
items.description: In this exclusive interview with Tehran Times, Greek political influencer and activist Sissy Fragho explains why she decided to travel to Iran, how her perception of the country changed over time, and why she says she is willing to face criticism and the loss of followers for speaking out on issues she believes in.

items.title: North Korea reaffirms support for Moscow over Ukraine
items.description: North Korea reaffirmed its support for Russia’s war on Ukraine as the nations’ foreign ministers met in Moscow, Pyongyang’s state media reported Tuesday, increasing speculation Kim Jong Un may soon visit the Kremlin. Diplomatically isolated North Korea and Russia have rapidly expanded cooperation since Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in early 2022. Pyongyang has sent troops to Russia’s embattled Kursk region and supplied Moscow with weapons systems, in return securing financial assistance, food, energy supplies and military technology, analysts say. Following a Moscow meeting between North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui and her Russian counterpart Sergey Lavrov, Pyongyang’s state media said the nations had “reaffirmed their steadfast political will to accelerate the long-term multi-faceted development of the bilateral relations”. “The DPRK side expressed invariable support for all the internal and external policies of the Russian Federation to eliminate the root cause of the Ukrainian dispute and defend its national sovereignty, territorial integrity and international justice,” state news agency KCNA said. The agency reported that Russia, meanwhile, expressed full support for North Korea’s efforts to defend its sovereignty and security. “I would like once again to express my gratitude for the support provided to the special military operation we are conducting,” Putin said in a video released by the Kremlin on Sunday. Choe laid a wreath at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldiers by the wall of the Kremlin on her visit, KCNA said Tuesday. Kim Jong Un last travelled to Russia in September 2023, and Putin made a rare visit to Pyongyang in June 2024. Choe’s visit to Moscow could have been aimed at discussing a trip to Russia by Kim later this year, according to South Korean media reports. “Putin has repeatedly invited Kim to visit Russia since his trip to Pyongyang in 2024,” Park Won-gon, a professor of North Korean studies at Ewha Womans University, told AFP. Chinese president Xi Jinping made a rare trip to Pyongyang last month, where he and Kim pledged to bolster ties. While Pyongyang has drawn closer to Moscow, China remains North Korea’s largest economic partner, accounting for nearly 98 percent of the country’s foreign trade in 2024, according to official figures. “After hosting Chinese President Xi Jinping this year, Kim would have a strong incentive to visit Russia as well,” Park said. “It would showcase North Korea breaking out of diplomatic isolation while highlighting support from both China and Russia, reinforcing the image of a North Korea-China-Russia alignment.” The post North Korea reaffirms support for Moscow over Ukraine appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: VOS RÉACTIONS-Sénégal: l'ancien président Macky Sall obtient finalement le soutien de son pays à sa candidature onusienne
items.description: Trois jours après un entretien controversé avec Macky Sall à Dakar, Bassirou Diomaye Faye a tranché : le Sénégal apporte son plein soutien à la candidature de son prédécesseur au poste de secrétaire général des Nations unies. Que pensez-vous de cette décision ? Risque-t-elle d’être perçue comme une trahison dans le propre camp de Bassirou Diomaye Faye ? Le chef de l'État cherche-t-il déjà des alliances dans l’optique de la présidentielle de 2029 ?

items.title: ‘My football dream ended after coach abused me,’ Peller claims
items.description: TikTok content creator Habeeb Hamzat, popularly known as Peller, has alleged that he was sexually abused by a football coach as a teenager, saying the traumatic experience brought an end to what he believed could have been a successful football career. Speaking on the latest episode of The Honest Bunch Podcast, published on YouTube on Monday, Peller said the alleged incidents occurred when he was between 14 and 15 years old. According to him, the experience forced him to abandon his ambition of becoming a professional footballer. “I’m supposed to be at the World Cup currently,” he said. Peller said the team usually assembled at an uncompleted building before heading to the football pitch for training. According to him, players who arrived late were often fined, but he was not always able to pay, making him a frequent target for the coach. “He would ask the other players to go to the field and tell me to stay behind because I came late. Then he would call me closer and start pressing my breasts,” Peller alleged. He also recounted an incident in Agric, Ikorodu, where the coach allegedly invited him under the pretext of helping him collect an item. According to Peller, when he arrived at the coach’s one-room apartment and handed over the item, the coach allegedly removed his clothes and asked him to get onto the bed. “I told him, ‘Come to the bed? I only came to bring what you asked for,'” he recalled. Peller claimed the coach attempted to persuade him by promising to give him more playing time. “He told me not to worry and that he would allow me to play full matches,” he said. The TikTok star said he later stepped outside after noticing some children playing football nearby and joined them briefly, a move that reportedly angered the coach. According to him, the coach called him back into the room and locked the door. Sensing danger, Peller said he told the coach he needed to urinate. He claimed that as soon as the door was unlocked, he seized the opportunity to flee and never returned. He said the experience forced him to abandon football and eventually led him into content creation. “That was when I became wise and started doing my tap, tap, tap,” he said, referring to his transition into livestreaming. Peller also reflected on the early days of his online career, saying he attracted only two or three viewers before meeting fellow content creator Jarvis, whose collaborations helped boost his popularity. The post ‘My football dream ended after coach abused me,’ Peller claims appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: La France s’apprête à interdire les réseaux sociaux aux moins de 15 ans
items.description: La France a franchi, lundi, une étape décisive vers l'interdiction de l’accès aux réseaux sociaux pour les moins de 15 ans, après l'adoption en commission d'une proposition de loi destinée à mieux protéger les mineurs face aux risques liés aux plateformes numériques. Le texte, qui prévoit l’interdiction de l’ensemble des réseaux sociaux aux enfants de moins de 15 ans, sans distinction entre les différentes plateformes, doit désormais être soumis à un vote définitif des deux chambres avant sa promulgation. Le gouvernement français ambitionne une entrée en vigueur du dispositif dès la rentrée scolaire de septembre. Cette loi s’inscrit dans un contexte de préoccupations croissantes en France concernant les effets des réseaux sociaux sur la santé mentale des adolescents, le cyberharcèlement, l’exposition à des contenus violents ou inappropriés et les mécanismes d’addiction liés aux algorithmes des plateformes. Le texte prévoit que les plateformes devront mettre en place des dispositifs de vérification de l’âge afin d’empêcher les moins de 15 ans d’accéder à leurs services, un point qui reste toutefois sensible au regard du droit européen et des exigences en matière de protection des données personnelles. La Commission européenne avait en effet demandé plusieurs ajustements afin d’assurer la compatibilité de la législation française avec le règlement européen sur les services numériques (Digital Services Act), tout en reconnaissant la possibilité pour les États membres de fixer un âge minimal d’accès aux réseaux sociaux sous certaines conditions. L’initiative française est suivie avec attention à l’échelle européenne où plusieurs États membres envisagent un renforcement de la protection des mineurs en ligne, tandis que Bruxelles travaille à une approche harmonisée sur la vérification de l’âge et la sécurité des enfants sur les plateformes numériques. The post La France s’apprête à interdire les réseaux sociaux aux moins de 15 ans appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .
